questions.q: What is the formal English name of the mission popularly known as Mangalyaan?
questions.explanation: Mangalyaan is the popular name for the Mars Orbiter Mission (MOM), an orbiter sent to study Mars from orbit.

questions.q: Which space agency built and operated the Mars Orbiter Mission?
questions.explanation: The Mars Orbiter Mission was developed and operated by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).

questions.q: What does the Hindi-derived name 'Mangalyaan' literally mean?
questions.explanation: 'Mangal' means Mars and 'yaan' means craft or vehicle, so Mangalyaan means 'Mars-craft'.

questions.q: In which year was the Mars Orbiter Mission launched?
questions.explanation: The Mars Orbiter Mission was launched on 5 November 2013.

questions.q: What major 'first' did India achieve with the Mars Orbiter Mission?
options: First country to land a rover on Mars
First country to reach Mars orbit on its very first attempt
First country to send humans to Mars
First country to return a sample from Mars
questions.explanation: India became the first country to successfully reach Mars orbit on its very first attempt.

questions.q: Which planet did Mangalyaan travel to and orbit?
questions.explanation: Mangalyaan (Mars Orbiter Mission) was sent to orbit and study the planet Mars.

questions.q: Was the Mars Orbiter Mission an orbiter, a lander, or a rover?
questions.explanation: Mangalyaan was an orbiter, designed to circle Mars and observe it from orbit rather than land on the surface.

questions.q: One of the most celebrated features of the Mars Orbiter Mission was that it was:
options: Extremely low cost compared with other Mars missions
questions.explanation: The mission was widely praised for its remarkably low cost compared with other nations' Mars missions.

questions.q: Reaching Mars made India the first nation in which continent's region to do so?
questions.explanation: India became the first Asian nation to successfully reach Mars orbit.

questions.q: The Mars Orbiter Mission was primarily described as what kind of mission?
options: A technology demonstration and science mission
questions.explanation: ISRO designed MOM mainly as a technology demonstrator for interplanetary travel, with a secondary science component.

questions.q: On what date did the Mars Orbiter Mission successfully enter orbit around Mars?
questions.explanation: Mangalyaan was successfully inserted into Mars orbit on 24 September 2014.

questions.q: Which launch vehicle carried the Mars Orbiter Mission into space?
questions.explanation: The mission was launched aboard a Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle, designated PSLV-C25.

questions.q: From which launch site was the Mars Orbiter Mission launched?
questions.explanation: Mangalyaan lifted off from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre at Sriharikota in Andhra Pradesh.

questions.q: Roughly how much did the Mars Orbiter Mission cost, a figure often cited to highlight its frugality?
questions.explanation: The mission cost about 450 crore rupees (roughly 74 million US dollars), far less than comparable Mars missions.

questions.q: Why did the spacecraft initially orbit Earth for several weeks before heading to Mars?
options: To gradually raise its orbit using engine burns before the trans-Mars injection
To wait for a crew to dock with it
questions.explanation: ISRO used a series of orbit-raising burns around Earth to build up speed before the trans-Mars injection toward Mars.

questions.q: Approximately how long did the journey from Earth to Mars take for the orbiter?
questions.explanation: The spacecraft cruised for roughly ten months between its November 2013 launch and its September 2014 Mars arrival.

questions.q: Which of these was one of the scientific instruments carried aboard the Mars Orbiter Mission?
options: A drill for collecting rock cores
A seismometer planted on the surface
questions.explanation: The orbiter carried a Methane Sensor for Mars (MSM) among its five scientific payloads.

questions.q: Approximately how many scientific instruments (payloads) did the Mars Orbiter Mission carry?
questions.explanation: MOM carried five scientific payloads with a total mass of about 15 kilograms.

questions.q: Which camera aboard the orbiter returned widely shared colour images of the full disc of Mars?
questions.explanation: The Mars Colour Camera (MCC) captured striking full-disc colour images of Mars that were widely circulated.

questions.q: Who was the Chairman of ISRO at the time of the Mars Orbiter Mission's successful Mars orbit insertion in 2014?
questions.explanation: K. Radhakrishnan was the ISRO Chairman when Mangalyaan successfully entered Mars orbit in September 2014.

questions.q: What was the originally planned design lifetime of the Mars Orbiter Mission in orbit?
questions.explanation: The orbiter was designed for a mission life of about six months, though it ended up operating far longer.

questions.q: Photographs from mission control featured prominently in news coverage; what was notable about the Mars Orbiter Mission team?
options: It included many women scientists and engineers
It was made up entirely of foreign experts
It consisted of only three people
It worked entirely remotely from abroad
questions.explanation: Photographs of the mission's women scientists and engineers celebrating became iconic symbols of the mission.

questions.q: The Mars Orbiter Mission was launched during a particularly favourable alignment that occurs roughly every:
questions.explanation: Favourable Earth-Mars launch windows recur roughly every 26 months, when the planets are well positioned for transfer.

questions.q: Which liquid-fuel engine performed the critical Mars Orbit Insertion burn for Mangalyaan?
questions.explanation: The spacecraft's 440-newton Liquid Apogee Motor (LAM) fired to slow the craft into Mars orbit.

questions.q: Before the orbit insertion burn, the main engine had been idle for many months and was test-fired shortly beforehand. About how long had it been dormant?
questions.explanation: The Liquid Apogee Motor had been dormant for around 300 days of cruise and was test-fired before the critical insertion burn.

questions.q: The Mars Orbiter Mission was placed into what kind of orbit around Mars?
options: A polar orbit grazing the surface
questions.explanation: Mangalyaan was placed in a highly elliptical orbit, ranging from a few hundred kilometres to tens of thousands of kilometres from Mars.

questions.q: Approximately how much did the Mars Orbiter spacecraft weigh at launch (including fuel)?
questions.explanation: The spacecraft had a launch mass of roughly 1,350 kilograms, most of which was propellant.

questions.q: Which of these scientific goals was a key objective of the Mars Orbiter Mission's instruments?
options: Searching the Martian atmosphere for methane
questions.explanation: Studying the Martian atmosphere, including searching for methane, was a central scientific objective of the orbiter.

questions.q: ISRO finally lost communication with the long-lived orbiter and declared it had ended around which year, after far exceeding its planned life?
questions.explanation: After operating for about eight years, ISRO indicated the orbiter had run out of propellant and lost contact, with the mission considered over around 2022.

questions.q: Mangalyaan's success placed India among an exclusive group; which entities had reached Mars before India?
options: Only the United States, the Soviet Union/Russia, and Europe (ESA)
No one had ever reached Mars before
questions.explanation: Before India, only the United States, the Soviet Union/Russia, and the European Space Agency had succeeded in reaching Mars.
